name: Agent Memory
description: Persistent memory system that lets agents recall past conversations, decisions, and context across sessions. Prevents repeated analysis and enables learning from past interactions.
author: LeadhuntAI
pros: Agents remember past decisions and avoid repeating mistakes
Lightweight SQLite-based storage, no external dependencies
Namespace-based isolation — each agent gets its own memory space
Automatic relevance scoring for memory retrieval
cons: Adds disk storage overhead (SQLite file per project)
Memory retrieval adds slight latency to each agent turn
Requires periodic memory cleanup to stay effective

apply_to.description: Which agents get memory capability — all agents or select specific ones

memory_backend.description: Storage backend — SQLite for performance, JSON for simplicity

rules_index_entries.summary: Agent memory system — persistent storage and recall of context across sessions

integration.post_install_message: Memory system installed. Agents will discover it via RULES_INDEX.md and can use the memory-recall skill.

name: BRAIN Memory System
description: Semantic long-term memory with hybrid routing, vector search, and tenant isolation. Gives coding agents persistent recall across sessions via MCP or REST API.
author: LeadhuntAI
pros: Semantic search finds relevant memories by meaning, not just keywords
Hybrid routing automatically categorizes memories into brain areas
Integrates with jCodeMunch for unified code + memory search
MCP server for native Claude Code integration
cons: Requires Qdrant vector DB (local mode or Docker)
Heavier setup than simple file-based memory systems

qdrant_mode.description: Qdrant storage mode — local (embedded, no Docker) or server (Docker)

rules_index_entries.summary: BRAIN memory system — semantic long-term memory with hybrid routing for coding agents

integration.post_install_message: BRAIN memory installed. Start the server with: python -m brain_blueprint.api_src.mcp_server
